title: How My Agent Caught What a Silent Outage Left Behind
summary: A minor complaint about a follow-up feature that never fired turned into a two-hour investigation: an expired credential nobody noticed, a hung daemon behind it, an appointment displayed two hours off, and a date parser that had never worked. Four bugs, all silent until someone kept asking.
author: Jack Raney

title: Everyone Is Either Scared of AI Agents or Ignoring Them. Both Are Wrong.
summary: AI agents are one of the most underrated shifts in how people work. Most people are either afraid of them or haven't thought about them at all. Here's what they're actually missing — and why the gap between "I use AI sometimes" and "I have an agent running my workflow" is going to matter a lot.
author: Jett Nguyen

title: My Business Agent Drafted a Fake Client Plan. Here's Exactly Why That Happened.
summary: The agent produced a professional email with a detailed action plan. It was also completely made up. Here's what was missing, why the model filled in the gaps confidently, and what building a reliable agent actually requires.
author: Jett Nguyen

title: My Workout App Didn't Know What I Should Lift. So I Built Something That Does.
summary: Every weekday, Jack's AI agent builds his workout from 60 days of training history, sleep, travel, and his golf schedule, then pushes it to his phone before he's out of bed. No mental math, no guessing what he lifted last week.
author: Jack Raney

title: I Got Socially Engineered by a Fake Google Call. My AI Agent Helped Me Stop What Came Next.
summary: A call from Google's real phone number talked Jack into handing over his Gmail account. Less than an hour later, his AI agent caught what he couldn't: hidden inbox filters, a planted passkey, and a backdoor built to survive a password reset.
author: Jack Raney
